title: Meta Platforms Inc. (META) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Meta Platforms Inc. (META). Economic Prospect Score: 81/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Meta's Year of Efficiency proved that Zuckerberg could cut costs without cutting growth. The advertising business is firing on all cylinders — Advantage+ AI-driven ad targeting has more than compensat...

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.0% WACC reflects Meta Platforms Inc.'s risk profile, including sector-specific volatility, competitive dynamics, and macroeconomic sensitivity.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate assumes Meta Platforms Inc. grows roughly in line with nominal GDP into perpetuity, reflecting the law of large numbers for a mature large-cap enterprise.

thesisParagraphs: Meta's Year of Efficiency proved that Zuckerberg could cut costs without cutting growth. The advertising business is firing on all cylinders — Advantage+ AI-driven ad targeting has more than compensated for Apple's ATT privacy changes, and Reels monetization is approaching Instagram feed levels.

The risk remains Reality Labs, which has burned $50B+ cumulatively with no clear consumer adoption timeline for the metaverse vision. My 12% growth rate assumes advertising continues to compound but applies a 10% discount rate as a 'Zuckerberg premium' — one man controls the entire company and can redirect tens of billions toward speculative bets at will.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: How does Opus handle the Reality Labs losses?
faq.answer: Reality Labs burns approximately $15B annually. The DCF treats this as ongoing capex that depresses FCF growth. If Meta shut down Reality Labs, the intrinsic value would increase by roughly $80-100 per share.

faq.question: Is Meta fairly valued at $615?
faq.answer: At approximately 12x FCF, Meta is one of the most reasonably valued mega-cap tech stocks. Opus's DCF suggests it's close to fair value, making it one of the few large-caps that isn't clearly overvalued.

faq.question: What's the biggest risk to Meta?
faq.answer: Regulatory. The EU's Digital Services Act, potential US regulation of social media for minors, and ongoing antitrust scrutiny could meaningfully impact both revenue and costs.

prospectVerdictDetail: Meta's advertising machine is the most efficient in digital media, powered by AI-driven targeting that keeps improving. The 3.2B daily active users across the family of apps create an unmatched audience. Reels monetization is closing the gap with TikTok. Reality Labs remains a $15B+ annual cash burn with uncertain returns, but the core business generates enough FCF to absorb this indefinitely.
prospectKeyRisks: Reality Labs cumulative losses exceeding $60B with no clear path to consumer-scale AR/VR adoption
Regulatory actions (EU DMA, US privacy legislation) restricting behavioral ad targeting could reduce ad effectiveness by 10-20%
TikTok maintaining its grip on Gen Z attention, permanently capturing the youngest demographic cohort
prospectKeyCatalysts: AI-driven ad targeting improvements driving 15-20% increase in advertiser ROAS
WhatsApp and Messenger monetization through business messaging reaching $5B+ ARR
Threads reaching 500M+ MAU and successfully monetizing as a Twitter/X replacement
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100.
